authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 4140.01, DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary materiel management directive for DoD supply availability and planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prerequisite validation and availability dependency mapping

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Governs lifecycle coordination boundaries and ownership shifts that affect defense supply and mission continuity workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.91, Product Support Management for the Adaptive Acquisition Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines support- and sustainment-facing planning obligations that inform supply continuity sequencing and readiness posture.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Specifies treatment and handling requirements for mission-sensitive records used in coordination and handoff artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence handling and handoff record integrity

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-161 Rev. 1, Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides a recognized framework to map supplier risk, dependencies, and evidence quality in supply-chain coordination decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 3364 Sector Context
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides defense-industry classification context to prevent generic logistics behavior.

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, mission criticality, available artifacts, and owner context before producing any routing or closure guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Dependency and owner mapping
workflow_stages.description: Build a dependency map across inventory, logistics, contract, and readiness systems and confirm destination owner availability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Conflict handling and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Detect missing evidence, duplicate owners, and policy conflicts; convert unresolved cases into blockers with explicit escalation output.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff execution and closure management
workflow_stages.description: Prepare closure confirmation only when required artifacts and acceptance conditions are present and owner mapping is complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and policy convergence
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant overrides when mission policy materially changes public-source assumptions and escalate when unresolved.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

artifacts: PO, RFQ, contract, and waiver artifacts
Source freshness logs and policy override evidence

allowed: Coordinate handoffs, shortages, and sourcing dependencies with explicit owner and artifact requirements.
Prepare evidence summaries, blocker logs, and escalation artifacts before routing onward.
Refuse execution-only requests and unsupported approvals while preserving required context.
escalate: Missing tenant policy context materially changes outcome
Legal, safety-critical, or financial authority is required and missing
Evidence conflicts across source systems cannot be reconciled
refuse: Request asks for procurement execution, freight control, or direct sourcing action
PO, contract, or waiver decisions are requested without authority
Closure is demanded without complete handoff acceptance artifacts

domain_failure_modes: Treating defense supply requests as generic procurement without mission-specific readiness and security constraints.
Marking handoff complete despite duplicate ownership or missing destination acceptance artifacts.
Ignoring vendor risk or ETA contradictions and issuing escalation without evidence confidence flags.
Overrunning adjacent authority boundaries for legal, safety, or finance decisions under urgency pressure.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Work handed off without required context

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant programs may define mission-essential urgency with different thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require explicit tenant severity mapping at runtime and block completion when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some sites accept conditional waiver with manager confirmation; others require legal review.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route as escalation with required waiver owner context before closure.

notes: Research output links named-defense materiel policy and recognized control guidance directly to coordination workflows.
Sources cover workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling dimensions required by the cross-industry research contract.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because each open point is converted into tenant-verified required context and escalation conditions.
